failure_reason: Requires xfs_io mremap command; FUSE mmap consistency and mremap ioctl not fully supported
Requires xfs_freeze (FIFREEZE ioctl); FUSE filesystems lack kernel-level freeze/thaw support
Requires AIO + preallocation; fallocate prealloc supported, FUSE AIO supported since Linux 4.2. Needs runtime validation with built xfstests (ltp/aio-stress binary required)
Requires Linux disk quota subsystem; FUSE filesystems do not integrate with kernel quota infrastructure

Mounted-path close analogues now prove ext4 FALLOC_FL_ZERO_RANGE and FS_IOC_FIEMAP separately (`ext4_fuse_fallocate_zero_range_zeroes_range`, `fuse_ioctl_fiemap_reports_valid_extents`). Keep allowlisted as likely-pass until the exact xfstests `fzero` + `fiemap` sequence is rerun end-to-end; this is no longer blocked on missing FIEMAP forwarding.
Requires scratch device with ext4 bigalloc mkfs feature; test infrastructure setup for FUSE-backed ext4 not available

Requires `chattr -e` to clear the system-managed EXTENTS flag and convert an extent file to the legacy non-extent format. FrankenFS now proves mounted-path EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS and user-settable EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS, but intentionally preserves EXT4_EXTENTS_FL, so this specific extent-conversion path remains unsupported.
Requires debugfs for direct inode corruption on raw device; incompatible with FUSE-mounted filesystem testing
